---
title: "Werkbank Museum of Design & Technology"
---

::: {.hero}
<span class="eyebrow">Design & technology, since 1991</span>

## Where objects tell the story of how we think

<p class="lede">The Werkbank Museum traces the history of design and technology through the everyday objects that shaped it — typewriters, circuit boards, chairs, telephones, and the machines that made them.</p>
:::

## Highlights from the collection

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
<span class="index-number">01</span>

### Typewriters to Trackpads
A century of how we've talked to machines, and how they've talked back.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
<span class="index-number">02</span>

### The Chair as Machine
Thonet to Aeron: seating as an engineering problem.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
<span class="index-number">03</span>

### Circuits & Silicon
From vacuum tubes to the first microprocessors, under glass.
:::

:::

[See the full collection →](exhibits.qmd)

## Plan your visit

Open Tuesday–Sunday, 10:00–18:00. [Hours, location, and tickets →](visit.qmd)
